records.content: 58 y/o male with T2DM diagnosed 8 years ago. Currently on metformin 1000mg BID. Last HbA1c 7.2%. Also has HTN on lisinopril and hyperlipidemia on atorvastatin. Vitals: BP 132/80, HR 74, BMI 30.5. Foot exam: intact sensation, no ulcers. Eye exam: last done 6 months ago — no retinopathy. Assessment: T2DM, suboptimal control. Plan: Continue metformin, recheck HbA1c in 3 months. Reinforce diet and exercise. Annual renal screening.

58 y/o male with T2DM. HbA1c improved to 7.0% with diet modifications. Renal function stable (eGFR 82). No hypoglycemic episodes. Foot exam normal. Assessment: T2DM, improved control. Plan: Continue metformin 1000mg BID. Next HbA1c in 3 months.

58 y/o male with T2DM. HbA1c drifting up to 7.3%. Renal function shows mild decline — eGFR 65 (was 88 a year ago). Creatinine trending up. Patient reports increased work stress, less exercise, poor dietary compliance. Assessment: T2DM, worsening control. CKD Stage 2, early. Plan: Reinforce lifestyle modifications. Consider adding SGLT2 inhibitor if HbA1c remains above target. Continue metformin. Recheck in 3 months.

59 y/o male with T2DM. HbA1c rising to 7.5%. Renal function continuing to decline — eGFR now 55 (CKD Stage 3a). Creatinine 1.5. Discussed with patient. Plan: Add empagliflozin 10mg daily (renoprotective + glucose lowering). Continue metformin 1000mg BID. Nephrology referral. Recheck labs in 3 months.

59 y/o male referred for progressive CKD. eGFR decline: 88 → 82 → 72 → 65 → 55 → 48 → 40 over 2 years. Likely diabetic nephropathy. Urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio 180 mg/g (moderately increased). Currently on metformin 1000mg BID, empagliflozin 10mg, lisinopril 20mg. Assessment: CKD Stage 3b, diabetic nephropathy. Plan: Continue empagliflozin (renoprotective). Continue lisinopril. BP target < 130/80. Low sodium diet. Follow-up in 3 months with labs. IMPORTANT NOTE: Monitor metformin — if eGFR drops below 30, metformin must be discontinued (risk of lactic acidosis).

59 y/o male with CKD. eGFR now 34 (Stage 3b, approaching Stage 4). Creatinine 2.0. Metabolic acidosis developing (bicarb 21). Potassium borderline elevated. Assessment: Progressive diabetic nephropathy. Plan: Start sodium bicarbonate 650mg TID for acidosis. Restrict potassium intake. Continue empagliflozin and lisinopril. Discussed need for close monitoring. Note: Metformin still appropriate at eGFR 34 but MUST be reduced to 500mg BID. Discussed with endocrinology.

60 y/o male with CKD. eGFR now 28 — Stage 4. Creatinine 2.3. Developing anemia of CKD (Hgb 11.2). Potassium elevated at 5.3. Assessment: CKD Stage 4 with complications. Plan: Start epoetin alfa for anemia. Kayexalate PRN for hyperkalemia. Dietary potassium restriction. Discuss AV fistula planning. Continue lisinopril and empagliflozin. CRITICAL: eGFR < 30 — METFORMIN MUST BE DISCONTINUED. Contacted endocrinology.

60 y/o male with T2DM and CKD Stage 4. HbA1c worsened to 7.8%. eGFR 25. Currently on metformin 500mg BID, empagliflozin 10mg. Patient reports he is still taking metformin as prescribed. Assessment: T2DM, worsening control in setting of advanced CKD. Plan: Continue current medications. Add insulin glargine 10 units at bedtime, titrate to fasting glucose < 130. Recheck HbA1c in 3 months.

records.indication: T2DM — adjunct to metformin and empagliflozin

ground_truth_issues.description: Metformin continued despite eGFR dropping below 30 mL/min. Nephrology explicitly noted in record #17 (July 2023) that metformin must be discontinued at eGFR < 30. However, endocrinology visit in October 2023 (record #19) continued metformin 500mg BID despite eGFR being 25. This is a FDA contraindication — metformin at eGFR < 30 carries significant risk of lactic acidosis, which can be fatal.
ground_truth_issues.clinical_reference: FDA Drug Safety Communication (2016): Metformin is contraindicated when eGFR falls below 30 mL/min/1.73m2. Should be reduced at eGFR 30-45 and discontinued below 30.
ground_truth_issues.what_should_have_happened: Dr. Liu should have discontinued metformin at the October 2023 visit when eGFR was 25. Alternative diabetes medications safe in severe CKD: insulin, certain DPP-4 inhibitors (linagliptin), or GLP-1 agonists with dose adjustment.
